marketState: neutral-to-bearish - storage surplus and bearish technicals outweigh a fading weather tailwind
summary: Front-month NG is at $2.907, essentially at fair value ($2.917, band $2.42-$3.42), so there is little pure valuation edge right now. Seasonality is weak evidence (Low reliability, 50pp spread across 16 years) but tilts bearish: price sits 11.3pp below its typical seasonal path, only 31% of years saw a higher price 4 weeks out, and the typical 4-week move is -3.1%. Near-term weather demand is running hot (+21.6% vs 5y, rangePos 0.93) which is supportive for the next several days, but the 8-16 day outlook fades back to roughly normal (+0.5% vs 5y) and the 24h demand trend is already declining. Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a sizeable +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, managed money remains net short (though only the 35th percentile, not an extreme), and technicals are bearish across the 1h-1w timeframes with the daily candle closing below its lower Bollinger Band. On balance the fundamental/technical weight of evidence (storage, technicals, weak-but-bearish seasonality) outweighs the fading weather tailwind, favoring a cautious bearish lean for the multi-week horizon, while the short-term picture is too mixed (hot weather vs. bearish daily trend, price already fair) to trade with conviction.

longTerm.stop: $3.05 (above +14D fair value $2.977 and above recent swing highs)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise, technicals are bearish across 1d/1w (below lower Bollinger, negative MACD, EMA50<200), and managed money is still net short. Seasonality adds weak bearish confirmation (31% prob higher in 4wks, -3.1% typical move) even though its reliability is Low.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained close back above $3.05-3.10 (above +14D fair value and the Nov contract level), or a storage deficit surprise / renewed hot-weather escalation into the 8-16 day window that flips demand_8_16D materially above 5y normal.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is already essentially fair (spread -$0.010 now, -$0.045 at +7D) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and signals conflict: hot 1-7 day weather demand (+21.6% vs 5y) is bullish while the daily trend is bearish and oversold (below lower Bollinger, RSI 34.7) and lower timeframes (5m/15m) are flat/neutral.
shortTerm.trigger: Long trigger: price reclaims $2.95-$3.00 with 1h/4h MACD histogram turning positive, confirming a weather-driven bounce toward $3.05-3.10. Short trigger: price breaks and holds below $2.85 with RSI continuing to fall, confirming continuation toward the $2.75 fair-value zone.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise
Daily/weekly technicals bearish: price below lower Bollinger Band, negative MACD, EMA50<200
Seasonality weak (Low reliability) but tilted bearish: only 31% probability of higher price in 4 weeks, -3.1% typical move
Near-term weather demand hot (+21.6% vs 5y, rangePos 0.93) but fading toward normal by day 8-16
Price essentially at fair value now ($2.907 vs $2.917 fair) - little standalone mispricing edge
risks: Hot near-term weather could intensify further (heat wave persistence, tropical activity) driving a sharp short-covering rally against the bearish thesis
Managed-money short positioning is not extreme (35th percentile), leaving room for shorts to add pressure but also limiting a forced-squeeze bullish catalyst
Futures curve shows a normal steep winter contango (Dec $3.79, Jan $4.24) - any early repricing of winter risk premium could lift the front month unexpectedly
